rationale.R1.2: The conclusion asserts 'uniformly superior' performance, but the body reports a ~9% ITAE regression on Plant E, essentially identical results on Plant C, and a withheld baseline for Plant D; logical coherence between body and conclusion is central under a substance-check reading.
rationale.R1.3: Three of five plants yield weak, null, or negative results against the opening claim of 'consistent improvements across all benchmarks'; checking whether tabular evidence supports the contribution claim is load-bearing if 'check' means verifying accuracy.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is the plausible alternative load-bearing interpretation of 'check this'; the prose contains a suspicious in-text data omission ('omit for space') and hedged language that conflicts with the assertive framing — both prime targets of a proofread reading.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I check the logical and evidential accuracy of the claims — e.g., whether the reported numbers actually support 'uniformly superior across all benchmarks' — or do you want writing-quality feedback (grammar, clarity, flow)?
